scenario: 📅 Small Talk: Yesterday — Asking about the weekend: 'What did you get up to?'
text:
🎧 YouGlish
To ask a coworker about their weekend or yesterday casually, use the phrasal verb: {{c1::"What did you get up to over the weekend?"}}
explanation:
Native speed: 'wʌ·dʒə gɛ·tʌp·tə...'
Rules applied:
• what did you → /wʌ·dʒə/ (Yod Coalescence: /d/ + /j/ → /dʒ/ + schwa reduction)
• get up → /gɛ·tʌp/ (Flap T cross-boundary: /t/ of 'get' is flapped between /ɛ/ and /ʌ/ → /gɛ·ɾʌp/)
• to → /tə/ (schwa reduction)

🧠 'Get up to' is a very common colloquial phrasal verb meaning 'to do' (specifically referring to leisure activities).
usage:
• "What did you get up to yesterday? Anything fun?"
  • "What did you guys get up to last night?"
spanish: '¿Qué hiciste el fin de semana?' — 'Get up to' es un phrasal verb coloquial equivalente a 'hacer' en el tiempo libre. Fonética: 'what did you' colapsa a 'wah-juh', 'get up' se une por Flap T y 'to' se debilita a schwa.

scenario: 📅 Small Talk: Yesterday — Answering: 'I just hung out'
text:
🎧 YouGlish
If you had a quiet day doing nothing special, the most natural native response is: {{c1::"Not much, I just hung out at home and took it easy."}}
explanation:
Native speed: 'nɑt mʌtʃ, aɪ dʒʌs hʌ·ŋaʊ·ɾət hoʊm ən tʊ·kɪ·ɾiː·zi'
• just hung → /dʒʌs hʌŋ/ (consonant elision: final /t/ of 'just' is omitted before 'hung')
• hung out at → /hʌ·ŋaʊ·ɾət/ (linking C+V: /ŋ/ links to 'out', and /t/ of 'out' is flapped [ɾ] before 'at')
• took it easy → /tʊ·kɪ·ɾiː·zi/ (linking + Flap T: /k/ of 'took' links to 'it', and /t/ of 'it' flaps before 'easy')

🧠 'Hung out' (phrasal verb 'hang out' in past) means spent time relaxing. 'Took it easy' means rested.
usage:
• "I just hung out with some friends on Saturday."
  • "I spent the whole day taking it easy after a crazy work week."
spanish: 'No mucho, solo pasé el rato en casa y me lo tomé con calma.' — Respuesta para un día tranquilo. Fonética: la /t/ de 'just' desaparece, 'hung out' se enlaza y se genera un Flap T en 'out at', igual que en 'took it easy' ('tu-ki-ri-zi').
